methodology: Refactored to Empowered-style OKRs: a small set of company objectives, linked source objectives, source-level key results, explicit team ownership, and limited use of High Integrity Commitments.
okrDesignPrinciples: Objectives describe meaningful business and customer outcomes, not feature delivery.
Key results measure source objectives, while source objectives map upward into a small set of company objectives.
Every key result names executing and supporting teams so accountability is explicit.
High Integrity Commitments are used sparingly and only for work that is materially deadline-sensitive.

companyObjectives.title: 2026-Q1 Make execution reliably repeatable
companyObjectives.statement: Improve the operating system behind the product so launches, service recovery, reliability, and cross-functional delivery become predictably repeatable.
companyObjectives.okrDesignNotes: Keeps teams accountable for operational outcomes and avoids turning roadmap delivery into a checklist of feature shipments.

inspiredByInsights.howItInspiresObjective: Provides evidence and product context for the objective's operations tradeoffs.

inspiredByInsights.insightTitle: Parking control tower convergence: validated discovery
inspiredByInsights.howItInspiresObjective: Provides evidence and product context for the objective's operations tradeoffs.

companyObjectives.title: 2026-Q1 Turn product progress into economic leverage
companyObjectives.statement: Translate better customer and operational outcomes into stronger revenue quality, retention, and strategic leverage for the business.
companyObjectives.okrDesignNotes: Separates commercial results from activity metrics so leaders can evaluate whether product work is compounding into business value.

inspiredByInsights.howItInspiresObjective: Provides evidence and product context for the objective's leverage tradeoffs.

objectives.title: 2026-Q1 Make parking operations feel like one controlled system
objectives.objective: Reduce parking-operator friction by converging pricing, payment, access, and enforcement workflows into a clearer control-tower model.

linkedCustomerKPIs.description: Share of parked vehicles converted into valid paid sessions or permits.
Time needed to process and close parking violations or permit disputes.

linkedBusinessKPIs.description: Recurring software and services revenue from parking operator accounts.

keyResults.target: Turn more parking events into valid, policy-compliant sessions
keyResults.baseline: Conversion is hurt by fragmented channel and access behaviors
keyResults.statement: Increase paid session conversion rate by making tariffs, payments, permits, and access behave consistently across the parking estate.

executingTeams.intent: Lead the work to improve paid session conversion rate by making tariffs, payments, permits, and access behave consistently across the parking estate via Parking Configuration & Policy Management and Dynamic Pricing & Occupancy Optimization.

supportingTeams.intent: Support the KR by contributing capability for paid session conversion rate, aligned with its focus on own permits, access-control, LPR, and parking-compliance workflows across channels and sites via Access, Permit & LPR Control and Enforcement & Compliance Workflows.

Support the KR by contributing capability for paid session conversion rate, aligned with its focus on own the shared ledger, settlement, and reconciliation backbone across parking, transit, business, and partners via Settlement, Ledger & Revenue Reconciliation.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: This KR should be pursued as an outcome target with room for discovery, iteration, and tradeoff.

target1.label: 2026-Q2 target
target1.target: Turn more parking events into valid, policy-compliant sessions
target1.projectedValue: Current estimate: Paid session conversion rate moves far enough to show clear momentum toward turn more parking events into valid, policy-compliant sessions, driven by increase paid session conversion rate by making tariffs, payments, permits, and access behave consistently across the parking estate.

target2.label: 2026-year-end target
target2.target: Turn more parking events into valid, policy-compliant sessions
target2.projectedValue: Current estimate: Paid session conversion rate ends the horizon sustainably ahead of today's baseline, with turn more parking events into valid, policy-compliant sessions holding across the broader operating model.

keyResults.baseline: Resolution slows down when evidence and workflow states are fragmented
keyResults.statement: Reduce violation resolution cycle time by standardizing compliance workflows and evidence handling.

executingTeams.intent: Lead the work to reduce violation resolution cycle time by standardizing compliance workflows and evidence handling via Access, Permit & LPR Control and Enforcement & Compliance Workflows.

supportingTeams.intent: Support the KR by contributing capability for violation resolution cycle time, aligned with its focus on own workflow, case, exception, and communication foundations reused across all operations-heavy business lines via Workflow, Case & Exception Management and Communications & Notifications.

Support the KR by contributing capability for violation resolution cycle time, aligned with its focus on own zone configuration, tariff logic, session control, and asset monetization workflows for parking operators via Parking Configuration & Policy Management and Dynamic Pricing & Occupancy Optimization.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: This KR is deadline-sensitive enough that the organization should manage it as a commitment rather than a stretch outcome.

target1.label: 2026-Q2 target
target1.projectedValue: Current estimate: Violation resolution cycle time moves far enough to show clear momentum toward resolve parking compliance issues faster, driven by reduce violation resolution cycle time by standardizing compliance workflows and evidence handling.

target2.label: 2026-year-end target
target2.projectedValue: Current estimate: Violation resolution cycle time ends the horizon sustainably lower than today's baseline, with resolve parking compliance issues faster holding across the broader operating model.

keyResults.target: Strengthen expansion and retention inside parking accounts
keyResults.baseline: ARR growth depends on making the platform feel more operationally coherent
keyResults.statement: Increase parking platform ARR by improving the operational-control story for parking operators.

executingTeams.intent: Lead the work to improve parking platform ARR by improving the operational-control story for parking operators via Parking Configuration & Policy Management and Dynamic Pricing & Occupancy Optimization.

supportingTeams.intent: Support the KR by contributing capability for parking platform ARR, aligned with its focus on own permits, access-control, LPR, and parking-compliance workflows across channels and sites via Access, Permit & LPR Control and Enforcement & Compliance Workflows.

Support the KR by contributing capability for parking platform ARR, aligned with its focus on own the shared ledger, settlement, and reconciliation backbone across parking, transit, business, and partners via Settlement, Ledger & Revenue Reconciliation.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: This KR should be pursued as an outcome target with room for discovery, iteration, and tradeoff.

target1.label: 2026-Q2 target
target1.target: Strengthen expansion and retention inside parking accounts
target1.projectedValue: Current estimate: Parking platform ARR moves far enough to show clear momentum toward strengthen expansion and retention inside parking accounts, driven by increase parking platform ARR by improving the operational-control story for parking operators.

target2.label: 2026-year-end target

target2.projectedValue: Current estimate: Parking platform ARR ends the horizon sustainably ahead of today's baseline, with strengthen expansion and retention inside parking accounts holding across the broader operating model.

objectives.title: 2026-Q1 Make transit open payment dependable enough to scale
objectives.objective: Reduce rollout risk in open-payment transport programs by improving device-state visibility, tap reliability, and financial exception handling.

linkedCustomerKPIs.description: Share of payment taps completed without error or fallback.
Share of journeys successfully charged under the intended fare rules.

linkedBusinessKPIs.description: Transaction volume processed through transit payment services.

keyResults.target: Make tap-and-go work reliably during live transport operations
keyResults.baseline: Success drops when device state and exception flows are not visible enough
keyResults.statement: Increase tap success rate by strengthening device-state control, payment retry behavior, and monitoring.

executingTeams.intent: Lead the work to improve tap success rate by strengthening device-state control, payment retry behavior, and monitoring via Transit Fare & Open Payment Engine.

supportingTeams.intent: Support the KR by contributing capability for tap success rate, aligned with its focus on own validator and field-device management, telemetry, and maintenance readiness via Device & Validator Management and Transit Operations Telemetry.

Support the KR by contributing capability for tap success rate, aligned with its focus on own the shared ledger, settlement, and reconciliation backbone across parking, transit, business, and partners via Settlement, Ledger & Revenue Reconciliation.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: This KR should be pursued as an outcome target with room for discovery, iteration, and tradeoff.

target2.projectedValue: Current estimate: Tap success rate ends the horizon sustainably ahead of today's baseline, with make tap-and-go work reliably during live transport operations holding across the broader operating model.

keyResults.baseline: Leakage is driven by payment exceptions and weak reconciliation transparency

executingTeams.intent: Lead the work to improve fare capture rate by tightening exception handling and settlement visibility via Transit Fare & Open Payment Engine.

objectives.title: 2026-Q1 Make the partner ecosystem easier to launch and trust
objectives.objective: Reduce ecosystem friction by standardizing partner launch, clarifying market rules, and improving post-launch performance visibility.

linkedCustomerKPIs.kpiName: Time to launch a new market
linkedCustomerKPIs.description: Elapsed time from decision to launch-ready market activation.
Share of partner-originated transactions completed without operational or financial failure.

linkedBusinessKPIs.description: Gross profit generated through partner-originated transactions and attach services.

keyResults.metric: Time to launch a new market
keyResults.target: Shorten onboarding and certification for ecosystem launches
keyResults.baseline: Launch time is slowed by rule ambiguity and service-led setup
keyResults.statement: Reduce time to launch a new market by turning partner onboarding into a reusable product workflow.

executingTeams.intent: Lead the work to reduce time to launch a new market by turning partner onboarding into a reusable product workflow via Partner API, Sandbox & Certification and Market Catalog & Operator Rules.

supportingTeams.intent: Support the KR by contributing capability for time to launch a new market, aligned with its focus on own identity, external API governance, and developer-platform capabilities reused across Arrive products via Identity & Access Management and API Gateway & Developer Experience.

Support the KR by contributing capability for time to launch a new market, aligned with its focus on own partner enablement, launch operations, and partner performance reporting via Partner Analytics & Revenue Reporting.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: This KR is deadline-sensitive enough that the organization should manage it as a commitment rather than a stretch outcome.

target1.label: 2026-Q2 target
target1.target: Shorten onboarding and certification for ecosystem launches
target1.projectedValue: Current estimate: Time to launch a new market moves far enough to show clear momentum toward shorten onboarding and certification for ecosystem launches, driven by reduce time to launch a new market by turning partner onboarding into a reusable product workflow.

target2.label: 2026-year-end target

target2.projectedValue: Current estimate: Time to launch a new market ends the horizon sustainably lower than today's baseline, with shorten onboarding and certification for ecosystem launches holding across the broader operating model.

supportingTeams.intent: Support the KR by contributing capability for transaction success rate, aligned with its focus on own identity, external API governance, and developer-platform capabilities reused across Arrive products via Identity & Access Management and API Gateway & Developer Experience.

Support the KR by contributing capability for transaction success rate, aligned with its focus on own partner enablement, launch operations, and partner performance reporting via Partner Analytics & Revenue Reporting.

Support the KR by contributing capability for transaction success rate, aligned with its focus on own in-car and automotive partner capabilities that extend parking and mobility services into connected-vehicle journeys via Automotive & In-Car Data Services.

keyResults.commitmentRationale: This KR is deadline-sensitive enough that the organization should manage it as a commitment rather than a stretch outcome.
